title: narcissus linnaeanus var. barlae
intro: Narcissus linnaeanus var. barlae, also known as Barlae's narcissus or Barla's daffodil, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including the presence of alkaloids such as galantamine, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Narcissus linnaeanus var. barlae include its potential to improve memory and cognitive function, as well as its possible use in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of dried flowers, leaves, and bulbs, which can be decocted in water to create a medicinal tea.
